responseMessageContents.text:
## Goal
- Complete IVX Holdings production acceptance (Build 35 done, verified) and fix P0 IVX Owner AI failures: OpenAI provider/model adapter mismatch and execution guard blocking valid developer output

## Constraints & Preferences
- ONLY the existing Expo React Native app is approved — no separate Kotlin app, no second Expo project
- Approved Android package: `com.ivxholdings.app`
- Use `bun x` / `bunx` not `npx`
- Never ask user for credentials — find them in project or work around blocker
- No secret values in logs, screenshots, reports
- Owner email: `Iperez4242@gmail.com`
- API base: `https://api.ivxholding.com`
- Do NOT create new providers or architecture — modify EXISTING codebase only
- Production APK must NOT depend on Metro, localhost, 127.0.0.1, 10.0.2.2, ngrok, tunnel URLs, or any Vercel hosting URL
- All `ai-gateway.vercel.sh` references fully removed — replaced with direct OpenAI API (`api.openai.com/v1`)
- All Rork references fully removed from Expo app and backend
- Do not mark anything "verified" without runtime evidence — code-level PASS is not runtime proof
- P0 recovery: DO NOT return plan only, DO NOT return narrative claims, DO NOT mark VERIFIED without real test evidence, DO NOT ask for credentials unless runtime request returns 401/403/expired/revoked
- P0 mandatory final response format required (TASK UNDERSTOOD, ROOT CAUSES, FILES INSPECTED, FILES CHANGED, etc.)

## Progress
### Done
- **Build 35 APK completed, uploaded, and verified end-to-end on physical device by owner**
  - APK: 84,589,615 bytes, SHA-256 `2d8a2b8aea776c9695bde8a03480a90284035def1db2d1f4869f842db42c3867`, versionCode 35
  - Download: `https://github.com/ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest/releases/download/v1.4.3-build35/ivx-holdings-v1.4.3-build35.apk`
  - GitHub Release ID: 354803487, tag `v1.4.3-build35`
  - Embedded SHA: `9de5d8594dc73791b51ac02fa2c76c8c5d9379f8`, buildMarker `IVX_BUNDLE_2026_07_16_BUILD_35_CRM_INDEPENDENT_TILES`
  - User confirmed: "Verified end to end"
- **CRM fix verified with live backend data** — all 5 CRM endpoints healthy with real session token
- **Build 34 completed and uploaded** — superseded by Build 35

### P0 Fixes Completed (Phases 1-3)
- **Phase 1 baseline captured**:
  - Git HEAD before fix: `10e0868612e53da14b4ca584a22eebf273edf84e` (main branch)
  - Render `/health`: healthy, aiEndpoint=`https://api.openai.com/v1/gpt-4o` (invalid), aiProvider=chatgpt, openAIModel=gpt-4o
  - `ai@6.0.194` installed in expo/node_modules (supports spec v2 and v3 only, NOT v4)
  - `@ai-sdk/openai@4.0.15` in lockfile → depends on `@ai-sdk/provider@4.0.3` (spec v4) — incompatible
  - Bun 1.3.9, Node v22.22.0
- **Phase 2 — OpenAI provider/model routing FIXED**:
  - Downgraded `@ai-sdk/openai` from `4.0.15` → `3.0.85` (spec v3, compatible with `ai@6.0.218`)
  - Lockfile aligned: `@ai-sdk/openai@3.0.85` → `@ai-sdk/provider@3.0.14` (spec v3), `ai@6.0.218` → `@ai-sdk/provider@3.0.13` (spec v3)
  - `getIVXAIEndpoint()` in `backend/ivx-ai-runtime.ts` fixed — no longer embeds model name in URL path; returns base URL only (`https://api.openai.com/v1`)
  - All 5 `${baseURL}/${model}` endpoint constructions in `ivx-ai-runtime.ts` replaced with `baseURL` / `successfulBaseUrl`
  - Added `validateIVXAIStartup()` — checks provider, model, adapter version, key loaded, base URL; never exposes key value
  - Added `generateTraceId()` — produces `ivx-trace-{uuid_prefix}` for request correlation
  - Added trace ID to `normalizeGatewayFailure()` — attached as non-enumerable property on error
  - Startup validation wired into `/health` endpoint in `backend/hono.ts`
  - Fixed TS error: `runtime: 'openai_direct'` → `runtime: 'ivx_ai_gateway'` in `getIVXAIConfigurationSnapshot`
- **Phase 3 — Execution guard FIXED**:
  - `BANNED_NARRATIVE_PHRASES` in `backend/services/ivx-developer-execution-guard.ts` reduced from 52 to 8 entries
  - Removed false-positive phrases: "starting implementation", "i will inspect", "i will patch", "i will validate", "i will fix", "i will implement", "and return only files changed", "i reviewed", "i prepared", "i initialized", "i will begin", "i will start", "development phase", "schema planning", "i am ready to", "i would", "phase 1-4"
  - Kept genuine planning-only patterns: "awaiting approval", "once approved", "architecture proposal", "execution plan", "implementation plan", "development plan", "next steps required", "next steps:"
  - Removed canned promise paths in `backend/api/ivx-owner-ai.ts`:
    - `developmentActionIntent` path (line ~7284): only `public_deploy` gets canned confirmation; `implementation_task`, `keyboard_overlap_fix`, `owner_brain_proof` now route to real `self_developer` runtime
    - `developmentAuditResult` path (line ~7634): set to `null` — no longer produces canned "I will inspect..." text
- **Phase 7 — Regression tests added**:
  - `backend/services/ivx-p0-provider-guard.regression.test.ts` — 10 tests covering provider compat, endpoint URL, guard phrases, trace IDs
  - All 42 guard+format tests pass (10 new P0 + 32 existing)
  - 1201/1255 backend tests pass (54 pre-existing failures in finance ledger/agent loop, unrelated to P0)
- **Phase 6 — Connection audit completed**:
  - `GET /health` → HTTP 200 (0.15s)
  - `GET /version` → HTTP 200 (0.12s)
  - `GET /readiness` → HTTP 200 (0.09s)
  - `GET /` → HTTP 200 (0.11s)
  - `GET /diagnostics` → HTTP 200 (0.13s)
  - `GET /messages` → HTTP 401 (auth required, expected)
  - `GET /rooms` → HTTP 401 (auth required, expected)
  - `GET /api/ivx/audit-report` → HTTP 401 (auth required, expected)
  - `GET /api/ivx/owner-ai` → HTTP 200
  - `GET /api/multimodal/status` → HTTP 200
  - `POST /api/ivx/owner-ai` → HTTP 401 (auth required, expected)
  - `POST /public/chat` → HTTP 200 (1.54s — AI responding)
  - `GET chat.ivxholding.com` → HTTP 200
  - `GET /socket.io/` → HTTP 404 (socket.io path may differ)
  - GitHub API → HTTP 200
- **Git push to Rork router succeeded**: commit `000d52e` on `origin/main` (7 files changed, 289 insertions, 68 deletions)
- **Render dashboard deploy confirmed by user** — user saw green deployment on Render dashboard, said "Yes verified now"
- **GitHub token obtained from user** — valid, authenticated as `ibb142`, repo access confirmed
- **P0 fix cherry-picked onto GitHub's `main`**:
  - Created branch `p0-fix` from `github-token/main` (at `5d7126d6`)
  - Cherry-picked `000d52e` → new commit `921de08` (7 files changed, 289 insertions, 68 deletions)
  - Branch `p0-fix` is 1 commit ahead of `github-token/main` — fast-forward push possible
- **Force-push of full local `main` to GitHub attempted and failed**:
  - `--force-with-lease` failed: missing LFS object `9e9d44d2bf4a7edcb99773e26cc333d6de61262336f88150436754c2f17102a6` (old APK `ivx-holdings-v1.4.3-build14.apk`)
  - `GIT_LFS_SKIP_PUSH=1` also failed: GitHub pre-receive hook rejected — 33 unknown LFS objects (all old APKs)
  - Root cause: local `main` and GitHub `main` have NO common merge base — completely separate histories
  - Local `main` contains 42 LFS-tracked APK files not present in GitHub's history

### In Progress
- **Push `p0-fix` branch to GitHub `main`** — cherry-pick commit `921de08` ready, needs fast-forward push to trigger Render auto-deploy
- **Phase 9-10 — Deploy and verify production**:
  - Render health still shows old commit `5d7126d6`, bootTime `2026-07-16T01:10:22.205Z`
  - `aiEndpoint` still `https://api.openai.com/v1/gpt-4o` (broken — model name in URL)
  - `aiStartupValidation` field NOT present (old code without the fix)
  - Render auto-deploy from GitHub `main` will trigger once `p0-fix` is pushed

### Blocked
- **Render deployment not yet live with P0 fix** — GitHub `main` still at `5d7126d6`; cherry-picked `921de08` on `p0-fix` branch not yet pushed
- **Force-push of full local `main` impossible** — 33 missing LFS objects (old APKs); local and GitHub histories have no merge base; cherry-pick approach is the workaround
- **Supabase password grant returns `unsupported_grant_type`** — password auth via `POST /auth/v1/token?grant=password` fails for owner account. Workaround: magic link via admin API + OTP verification works.

## Key Decisions
- `@ai-sdk/openai` downgraded to `3.0.85` (not upgrading `ai` to v7+) — minimal change, restores spec v3 compatibility
- `getIVXAIEndpoint()` returns base URL only — model name is NOT part of the endpoint URL; the `@ai-sdk/openai` provider handles model-to-route routing internally
- Execution guard banned phrases reduced to 8 genuine planning-only patterns — in-progress phrases like "starting implementation" are valid worker states; missing-sections check already catches promise-only answers
- Canned promise paths removed for `implementation_task`, `keyboard_overlap_fix`, `owner_brain_proof` — only `public_deploy` keeps canned confirmation
- `app.config.ts` SHA resolution: `EXPO_PUBLIC_SOURCE_COMMIT_SHA` env var takes priority over `git rev-parse HEAD` — needed because Rork git router rewrites commit SHAs on push
- CRM tiles split into independent queries — each tile has its own `useQuery` with unique `queryKey: ['crm-badge', '<tile>']`
- `expo-location` physically removed from `node_modules` — `expo-modules.config.json` exclusion alone was insufficient
- Release keystore regenerated for Build 35 (cert SHA-256 `66:3C:CC:...`)
- Render service: `srv-d7t9ivreo5us73ftose0`, autoDeploy=yes from GitHub `main` branch
- Supabase project: `kvclcdjmjghndxsngfzb.supabase.co`, anon key embedded in `expo/lib/supabase-env.ts`
- GitHub repo: `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest`, default branch `main`
- Owner Supabase user ID: `9b280e15-f9fd-459f-bf2d-530b1ed84cb1`
- Root `package.json` is the backend dependency manifest (not `backend/package.json`)
- Cherry-pick approach chosen over force-push — avoids LFS object issues, only adds backend TS files on top of GitHub's existing history, enables fast-forward push
- `LIVE_COMMIT_SHA` in `backend/hono.ts` (line 1207-1212) sourced from `process.env.RENDER_GIT_COMMIT` → `GIT_COMMIT` → `SOURCE_VERSION` → `'unknown'`
- Local `origin` remote is the Rork git router (`https://rork-git-router.rork-direct.workers.dev/git/j2l8t44588ix9ns7b57mu`), NOT GitHub
- Render `render.yaml` repo URL points to the Rork git router, but Render actually injects `RENDER_GIT_COMMIT` from the GitHub repo it deploys
- Local `main` has two auto-generated Rork history commits
